name: Tadalafil did not outperform placebo for FMD in a double-blind ED endothelial dysfunction RCT
statement: In a double-blind ED/endothelial dysfunction RCT, both tadalafil and placebo groups improved on brachial artery flow-mediated dilation after 4 weeks, and the between-group FMD change was not significant; adverse events were more frequent with tadalafil.
population_or_context: Men with ED and baseline FMD below 15%.
quantitative_note: FMD changed from 11.222 to 13.827 with tadalafil and from 11.617 to 14.027 with placebo; mean change tadalafil versus placebo was 2.426 versus 2.829, P=0.528. Adverse reactions were 14 with tadalafil versus 5 with placebo, P<0.001.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports pre/post FMD values, between-group P value, and adverse-reaction counts.

caveats: Exact dose was not specified in the abstract extract.
The trial does not address long-term cardiovascular outcomes.
population_profile: Men with ED and endothelial dysfunction; indirect for mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Low-dose tadalafil for 4 weeks; exact dose not specified in abstract extract.
effect_estimate: Mean FMD change 2.426 with tadalafil versus 2.829 with placebo; P=0.528.
safety_findings: Adverse reactions were more frequent with tadalafil than placebo in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for tadalafil endothelial surrogate effects in ED; low for healthy 30-50 cardiovascular benefit.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Large placebo response, short duration, ED selection, dose ambiguity at abstract level, and no event outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is ED population, short duration, and null between-group result.
